docs.title: AX Spec
docs.text:
Agent Experience (AX) specification and linting rules for building agent-friendly APIs.

AX Spec provides Spectral rules that enforce the DIRECT principles for APIs designed to be consumed by autonomous AI agents.

docs.title: Why AX?
docs.text:
Traditional APIs are designed for human developers who can interpret documentation, handle ambiguity, and debug errors manually. Agent Experience (AX) requires APIs that are:

Principle Description Deterministic Predictable behavior with strict schemas Introspectable Machine-readable capabilities and metadata Recoverable Structured errors with actionable suggestions Explicit All constraints declared in the specification Consistent Uniform patterns across endpoints Testable Safe sandbox environments for agent experimentation

docs.title: Using with Vacuum
docs.text:
# Install vacuum
brew install daveshanley/vacuum/vacuum

# Lint your OpenAPI spec with AX rules
vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/ax-openapi.json your-api.yaml

# Use a specific compliance level
vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/profiles/ax-l3-agent-ready.yaml your-api.yaml

docs.title: Using the CLI
docs.text:
# Build the CLI
go install github.com/plexusone/ax-spec/cmd/ax-spec@latest

# Lint your OpenAPI spec
ax-spec lint openapi.yaml

# Enrich spec with x-ax-* extensions
ax-spec enrich openapi.yaml -o openapi-ax.yaml

# Generate Go code from extensions
ax-spec gen openapi-ax.yaml -o pkg/ax

docs.title: Compliance Levels
docs.text:
AX Spec defines progressive compliance levels for incremental adoption:

Level Name Description AX-L1 Structured Valid OpenAPI with explicit types and operation IDs AX-L2 Deterministic Strict schemas, explicit required fields, no ambiguous types AX-L3 Agent-Ready Full AX extensions, structured errors, capability discovery

docs.text: These case studies document the integration of AX metadata into Go SDKs, demonstrating how machine-readable error codes, retry policies, and validation improve agent reliability.

docs.title: Common Patterns
docs.text:
Both integrations share common patterns:

  1. Error code constants — Typed constants for machine-readable errors
  2. Error metadata — Category, retryability, HTTP status mapping
  3. Retry policy mapping — All operations classified as safe/unsafe to retry
  4. Required field extraction — Pre-flight validation before API calls
  5. Capability mapping — Operation introspection for agents

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
This case study documents the integration of Agent Experience (AX) principles into elevenlabs-go, demonstrating how machine-readable metadata enables AI agents to handle errors, make retry decisions, and validate requests autonomously.

Key Results:

  • 9 error codes discovered via API probing
  • 236 operations mapped with retry policies
  • 72 operations have required field definitions
  • Error handling upgraded from string parsing to typed constants

docs.title: The Challenge
docs.text:
AI agents interact with APIs differently than human developers:

Aspect Human Developer AI Agent Error handling Read message, debug Parse structure, recover Retry logic Intuitive judgment Needs explicit rules Validation IDE warnings, docs Pre-flight checks Learning Read examples Execute iteratively

Traditional SDKs optimize for human ergonomics. Agent-friendly SDKs need machine-readable metadata.

docs.title: The Project
docs.text:
elevenlabs-go is a Go SDK for the ElevenLabs API:

  • 204 endpoints covering text-to-speech, voice cloning, dubbing, and more
  • 54,000 line OpenAPI specification
  • 330,000 lines of generated Go code (via ogen)
  • Wrapper layer providing ergonomic APIs over generated code

The SDK is used by AI agents for voice generation tasks.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When an agent encounters an API error:

{
  "detail": {
    "status": "document_not_found",
    "message": "The requested document was not found"
  }
}

The agent faces several challenges:

  1. How to identify the error? — Parse strings? Match substrings?
  2. Should it retry? — Safe for reads, dangerous for writes
  3. What alternatives exist? — No structured guidance
  4. Is the request valid? — Only learns after calling API

docs.title: Before AX Integration
docs.text:
// Fragile string matching
if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found") {
    // Handle... but which kind of "not found"?
}

// HTTP status codes lack specificity
if apiErr.StatusCode == 404 {
    // Is it a document? User? Workspace?
}

// No retry guidance
// Agent must guess or hardcode rules

docs.title: DIRECT Principles
docs.text:
The integration follows the DIRECT principles for Agent Experience:

Principle Implementation Deterministic Typed error constants with predictable values Introspectable Error metadata (category, retryability) Recoverable Category-based recovery strategies Explicit Required fields documented per operation Consistent Uniform error handling patterns Testable Comprehensive unit tests

docs.title: Step 1: API Discovery
docs.text:
The OpenAPI specification doesn't document all error codes. We used ax-spec's discovery feature to probe the actual API:

ax-spec enrich elevenlabs-openapi.json \
  --discover \
  --api-key $ELEVENLABS_API_KEY \
  --output elevenlabs-openapi-ax.json

Discovered Error Codes:

Code Category Description Retryable DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND not_found Resource doesn't exist No USER_NOT_FOUND not_found User doesn't exist No WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND not_found Workspace doesn't exist No NOT_LOGGED_IN auth User not authenticated No NEEDS_AUTHORIZATION auth Additional permissions required No INVALID_UID validation Invalid identifier format No UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY validation Request validation failed No MISSING_FEEDBACK validation Required feedback not provided No NO_EDIT_CHANGES validation Edit request had no changes No

docs.title: Step 2: Code Generation
docs.text:
Generated Go code from the enriched specification:

ax-spec gen elevenlabs-openapi-ax.json \
  --output ax/ \
  --package ax

Generated Files:

File Lines Purpose ax/doc.go 25 Package documentation ax/errors.go 140 Error constants and metadata ax/retry.go 260 Retry policies (236 operations) ax/validation.go 120 Required fields (72 operations) ax/capabilities.go 100 Operation capabilities ax/ax_test.go 180 Unit tests

docs.title: Step 3: SDK Integration
docs.text:
Enhanced the existing errors.go with AX-aware methods:

// Method on APIError to extract AX code
func (e *APIError) AXErrorCode() (string, bool) {
    for _, code := range ax.AllErrorCodes {
        if strings.Contains(e.Message, code) ||
           strings.Contains(e.Detail, code) {
            return code, true
        }
    }
    return "", false
}

// Top-level helpers
func IsAXError(err error, code string) bool
func GetAXErrorCode(err error) (string, bool)

docs.title: Error Handling Improvement
docs.text:
Before:

resp, err := client.Voices().Get(ctx, voiceID)
if err != nil {
    if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found") {
        // Fragile, might miss variations
        return nil, ErrVoiceNotFound
    }
    return nil, err
}

After:

resp, err := client.Voices().Get(ctx, voiceID)
if err != nil {
    if code, ok := elevenlabs.GetAXErrorCode(err); ok {
        switch code {
        case ax.ErrDocumentNotFound:
            // Specific, reliable match
            return nil, ErrVoiceNotFound
        case ax.ErrNeedsAuthorization:
            // Agent can request permissions
            return nil, ErrNeedsPermission
        case ax.ErrInvalidUID:
            // Agent can validate input
            return nil, ErrInvalidVoiceID
        }
    }
    return nil, err
}

docs.title: Retry Policy Coverage
docs.text:
236 operations now have documented retry safety:

// Safe to retry with exponential backoff
if ax.IsRetryable("get_voices") {
    return retry.Do(ctx, func() error {
        _, err := client.Voices().List(ctx)
        return err
    }, retry.WithBackoff(time.Second, 30*time.Second))
}

// Do not retry - would create duplicates
if !ax.IsRetryable("create_voice") {
    return err // Return immediately
}

Retry Policy Distribution:

Category Count Policy GET operations 98 Retryable List operations 22 Retryable POST (create) 48 Not retryable PUT (update) 26 Not retryable DELETE 26 Not retryable Special (POST reads) 16 Varies

docs.title: Pre-flight Validation
docs.text:
72 operations have required field definitions:

// Before calling API, validate required fields
fields := map[string]bool{
    "text": true,
    // Missing: voice_id (if required)
}

if msg := ax.ValidateFields("text_to_speech_full", fields); msg != "" {
    // msg = "missing required fields: ..."
    return fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %s", msg)
}

// All required fields present - safe to call
resp, err := client.TextToSpeech().Generate(ctx, req)

docs.title: Error Category Handling
docs.text:
Error metadata enables category-based recovery:

code, ok := elevenlabs.GetAXErrorCode(err)
if !ok {
    return err // Unknown error
}

info := ax.GetErrorInfo(code)

switch info.Category {
case "not_found":
    // Try alternative resource
    return tryAlternative(ctx)

case "auth":
    // Re-authenticate or request permissions
    return requestPermissions(ctx)

case "validation":
    // Fix request and retry
    fixedReq := fixRequest(req, code)
    return retryWith(ctx, fixedReq)
}

docs.title: API Coverage

docs.title: 1. API Discovery is Essential
docs.text:
The OpenAPI specification did not document all error codes. Real API calls were necessary to discover actual error responses.

Recommendation: Always probe production APIs to discover undocumented behavior.

docs.title: 2. Code Generation Scales
docs.text:
With 204 endpoints, manual metadata would be error-prone. Code generation from the enriched spec ensures consistency.

Recommendation: Invest in tooling that generates code from specifications.

docs.title: 3. Categories Enable Strategies
docs.text:
Individual error codes are useful, but categories enable broader strategies:

  • not_found → Try alternatives, report to user
  • auth → Re-authenticate, escalate permissions
  • validation → Fix input, retry

Recommendation: Define error categories, not just error codes.

docs.title: 4. Layers Work Together
docs.text:
The AX integration enhances the existing SDK without replacing it:

  • Foundation layer (ogen): Accurate API calls
  • Wrapper layer: Ergonomic human APIs
  • AX layer: Agent metadata and helpers

Recommendation: Add AX as a complementary layer, not a replacement.

docs.title: 5. Tests Validate Integration
docs.text:
Comprehensive tests ensure the integration works correctly across all scenarios.

Recommendation: Test error extraction, retry logic, and validation separately.

docs.title: Expanded Error Discovery
docs.text:
• Probe more endpoints systematically
  • Track error code changes over API versions
  • Automate discovery in CI/CD

docs.title: Idempotency Tracking
docs.text:
• Add x-ax-idempotent extension
  • Generate idempotency key helpers
  • Enable safe retries for more operations

docs.title: Cost Metadata
docs.text:
• Add x-ax-cost-estimate extension
  • Track credit usage per operation
  • Enable cost-aware agent decisions

docs.title: Capability Graphs
docs.text:
• Map related operations
  • Enable agent navigation
  • Support "what can I do next?" queries

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
The AX integration transforms elevenlabs-go from a human-centric SDK to an agent-friendly one:

Aspect Before After Error handling String parsing Typed constants Retry decisions Hardcoded 236 documented policies Validation Runtime errors Pre-flight checks Error semantics None Category metadata Agent reliability Guesswork Deterministic

By following DIRECT principles and using ax-spec tooling, we've demonstrated that existing SDKs can be enhanced for AI agent consumption with modest effort and significant value.

docs.title: Agent Reliability Improvement
docs.text:
Before AX:

Agent: Call API
API: Error "document not found"
Agent: Parse string... maybe retry?

API: Same error
Agent: Give up

After AX:

API: Error DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND

Agent: Try alternative document
Agent: Success

docs.title: Example: Complete Error Handling
docs.text:
resp, err := client.Voices().Get(ctx, voiceID)
if err != nil {
    if code, ok := elevenlabs.GetAXErrorCode(err); ok {
        info := ax.GetErrorInfo(code)

        log.Printf("Error: %s (category=%s, retryable=%v)",
            code, info.Category, info.Retryable)

        if info.Retryable {
            return retry(ctx, func() error { ... })
        }

        if ax.IsNotFoundError(code) {
            return useDefaultVoice(ctx)
        }
    }
    return err
}

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
This case study documents the integration of Agent Experience (AX) principles into opik-go, an LLM observability SDK. The integration enables AI agents to reliably trace their own behavior with machine-readable error handling, explicit retry policies, and pre-flight validation.

Key Results:

  • 19 domain-specific error codes defined
  • 201 operations mapped with retry policies
  • 67 operations have required field definitions
  • 7 capability types including streaming and evaluation

docs.title: The Observability Challenge
docs.text:
AI agents need to observe their own behavior to learn and improve. This creates a unique challenge: the observability system itself must be agent-friendly.

Aspect Human Developer AI Agent Error handling Read logs, debug Programmatic recovery Missing traces Manually investigate Auto-create resources Evaluation Review dashboards Run programmatically Retries Intuitive judgment Explicit policies needed

docs.title: The Project
docs.text:
opik-go is a Go SDK for Comet ML's Opik observability platform:

  • 201 API endpoints covering traces, spans, datasets, experiments, and evaluations
  • 14,820 line OpenAPI specification
  • LLM evaluation framework with heuristic and model-based scorers
  • Streaming support for large result sets
  • Used by AI agents to observe and improve their own behavior

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When tracing fails, agents face ambiguous errors:

{
  "status": 404,
  "message": "Not found"
}

Questions the agent cannot answer:

  1. What wasn't found? — Trace? Span? Dataset? Project?
  2. Should it retry? — Will it help or create duplicates?
  3. How to recover? — Create the resource? Use a different one?
  4. Is it permanent? — Or a transient failure?

docs.title: Before AX Integration
docs.text:
trace, err := client.GetTrace(ctx, traceID)
if err != nil {
    if apiErr, ok := err.(*APIError); ok {
        if apiErr.StatusCode == 404 {
            // Which resource is missing?
            // The trace? The project? The workspace?
            // No way to determine programmatically
        }
    }
    return nil, err
}

docs.title: Error Handling Improvement
docs.text:
Before:

trace, err := client.GetTrace(ctx, traceID)
if err != nil {
    // Generic error handling
    log.Printf("Error: %v", err)
    return nil, err
}

After:

trace, err := client.GetTrace(ctx, traceID)
if err != nil {
    code, ok := opik.GetAXErrorCode(err)
    if !ok {
        return nil, err
    }

    switch code {
    case ax.ErrTraceNotFound:
        // Create the trace first
        return client.CreateTrace(ctx, &Trace{ID: traceID, Name: "auto-created"})

    case ax.ErrProjectNotFound:
        // Create the project, then retry
        client.CreateProject(ctx, projectName)
        return client.GetTrace(ctx, traceID)

    case ax.ErrUnauthorized:
        // Re-authenticate
        return nil, ErrNeedsAuth

    case ax.ErrRateLimited:
        // Back off and retry
        time.Sleep(time.Second)
        return client.GetTrace(ctx, traceID)
    }

    return nil, err
}

docs.title: Self-Healing Tracing Pattern
docs.text:
func (a *Agent) recordAction(ctx context.Context, action Action) error {
    trace := &Trace{
        Name:  action.Name,
        Input: action.Input,
    }

    err := a.client.CreateTrace(ctx, trace)
    if err == nil {
        return nil
    }

    // Self-healing based on AX metadata
    info := opik.GetAXErrorInfo(err)
    if info == nil {
        return err
    }

    switch info.Category {
    case "not_found":
        // Create missing resource
        if code, _ := opik.GetAXErrorCode(err); code == ax.ErrProjectNotFound {
            a.client.CreateProject(ctx, a.projectName)
            return a.client.CreateTrace(ctx, trace)
        }

    case "rate_limit":
        // Exponential backoff (retryable)
        if info.Retryable {
            time.Sleep(time.Second * 2)
            return a.recordAction(ctx, action)
        }

    case "conflict":
        // Resource exists, fetch it instead
        existing, _ := a.client.GetTrace(ctx, trace.ID)
        return a.updateTrace(ctx, existing, action)
    }

    return err
}

docs.title: Pre-flight Validation
docs.text:
func validateRequest(operationID string, req interface{}) error {
    // Extract present fields via reflection or manual mapping
    present := extractPresentFields(req)

    if msg := ax.ValidateFields(operationID, present); msg != "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %s", msg)
    }
    return nil
}

// Usage
func (c *Client) CreateExperiment(ctx context.Context, req *ExperimentRequest) error {
    if err := validateRequest("createExperiment", req); err != nil {
        return err // Fail fast without API call
    }
    return c.api.CreateExperiment(ctx, req)
}

docs.title: 1. Observability Needs Precision
docs.text:
Generic "not found" errors are insufficient when agents trace themselves. Each resource type needs its own error code:

  • TRACE_NOT_FOUND — The trace doesn't exist
  • PROJECT_NOT_FOUND — The project doesn't exist (create it first)
  • SPAN_NOT_FOUND — The span doesn't exist (but trace might)

docs.title: 2. Self-Healing Patterns are Essential
docs.text:
Agents that observe themselves must handle their own tracing failures:

// Bad: Agent stops observing itself on first error
// Good: Agent recovers and continues observing

if code == ax.ErrProjectNotFound {
    createProject()
    retryTrace()
}

docs.title: 3. Domain-Specific Capabilities Matter
docs.text:
Observability has unique capabilities that generic CRUD doesn't capture:

  • CapEvaluate — LLM quality evaluation
  • CapStream — Large result streaming
  • CapAnalytics — Metrics and BI operations

docs.title: 4. Retry Policies Prevent Data Corruption
docs.text:
Observability data is append-only. Retrying creates risks:

createTrace (not retryable) — Would create duplicate traces
evaluateTraces (not retryable) — Would run evaluation twice
getTraceById (retryable) — Safe to retry reads

docs.title: 5. HTTP Status is Not Enough
docs.text:
Status 404 could mean:

  • Trace not found
  • Span not found
  • Project not found
  • Workspace not found
  • Any of 13 other "not found" conditions

AX error codes disambiguate completely.

docs.title: Comparison with elevenlabs-go
docs.text:
Aspect elevenlabs-go opik-go Domain Voice generation LLM observability Endpoints 204 201 Error codes 9 (API discovered) 19 (domain defined) Retry policies 236 201 Required fields 72 67 Special capabilities - Stream, Evaluate, Analytics Self-healing Media errors Tracing errors

Both SDKs benefit from AX, but the specific error codes and capabilities differ by domain.

docs.text: Run ax-spec discovery against the real Opik API to find additional error codes not documented in the spec.

docs.text: Add x-ax-idempotent extension support for safe retry of create operations with idempotency keys.

docs.text: Define patterns for partial success in batch operations (some items succeed, some fail).

docs.text: Expose evaluator capabilities (what metrics they produce, what inputs they need).

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
The AX integration transforms opik-go from a basic observability SDK to an agent-friendly one:

Aspect Before After Error handling HTTP status parsing 19 typed error codes Retry decisions Hardcoded or missing 201 operations mapped Validation Runtime API errors Pre-flight validation Capabilities Unknown Domain-specific discovery Agent behavior Fragile tracing Self-healing observability

For AI agents that need to observe themselves, reliable observability is foundational. AX makes that reliability achievable.

docs.text: Building Agent-Friendly LLM Observability SDKs

docs.title: The Challenge
docs.text:
AI agents need to observe their own behavior:

  • Trace LLM calls — Track inputs, outputs, latency
  • Record spans — Measure individual operations
  • Run evaluations — Score quality automatically
  • Handle errors — Recover from failures gracefully

Observability SDKs must be agent-friendly.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When an agent's tracing fails:

{
  "status": 404,
  "message": "Not found"
}

What can the agent do?

  • Is it a trace? Span? Dataset? Experiment?
  • Should it retry? Create the resource first?
  • How does it recover automatically?

docs.title: The Vision: Agent Experience (AX)
docs.text:
Design interfaces that enable agents to:

understand → call → recover → iterate

For observability: Agents must trace reliably to learn from their actions.

docs.title: DIRECT Principles for Observability
docs.text: Principle Observability Application Deterministic Same trace data → same recorded trace Introspectable Discover available metrics, evaluators Recoverable Know if trace failed, why, how to fix Explicit Required fields for spans, traces clear Consistent Uniform patterns across all resources Testable Mock tracing for unit tests

docs.title: The Project: opik-go
docs.text:
A Go SDK for Comet ML's Opik observability platform:

  • 14,820 line OpenAPI spec

docs.title: Why Observability Needs AX
docs.text:
Observability is critical for agent reliability:

Agent executes task
    ↓
Records trace → Error: TRACE_NOT_FOUND
    ↓
Without AX: Agent stalls, loses observability
With AX: Agent creates project first, retries

Self-observing agents need self-healing tracing.

docs.title: Retry Policy for Tracing
docs.text:
func recordSpan(ctx context.Context, span *Span) error {
    // Check if safe to retry
    if !ax.IsRetryable("createSpan") {
        // Not retryable - would create duplicates
        return client.CreateSpan(ctx, span)
    }

    // Safe operations can use retry logic
    return retry.Do(func() error {
        return client.CreateSpan(ctx, span)
    })
}

docs.title: Example: Self-Healing Tracing
docs.text:
func (a *Agent) recordAction(ctx context.Context, action Action) error {
    trace := &Trace{Name: action.Name, Input: action.Input}

    err := a.client.CreateTrace(ctx, trace)
    if err == nil {
        return nil
    }

    // Self-healing based on AX error code
    switch code, _ := opik.GetAXErrorCode(err); code {
    case ax.ErrProjectNotFound:
        a.client.CreateProject(ctx, a.projectName)
        return a.client.CreateTrace(ctx, trace)
    case ax.ErrRateLimited:
        time.Sleep(time.Second)
        return a.recordAction(ctx, action)
    default:
        return err
    }
}

docs.title: x-ax-* Extensions
docs.text: AX Spec defines OpenAPI extensions that provide machine-readable metadata for AI agents.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Extension Type Description x-ax-required-fields string[] Explicit list of fields required for operation x-ax-capabilities string[] Semantic capabilities for agent discovery x-ax-error-code string Machine-readable error code x-ax-error-suggestion string Actionable fix suggestion x-ax-retryable boolean Whether operation is safe to retry x-ax-idempotent boolean Whether repeated calls are safe x-ax-sandboxable boolean Whether safe to call in test environment x-ax-category string Functional grouping for discovery x-ax-cost-estimate object Resource consumption estimate

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: createPayment
      summary: Create a new payment
      x-ax-required-fields:
        - amount
        - currency
        - recipient_id
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - create_payment
        - transfer_funds
      x-ax-retryable: false
      x-ax-sandboxable: true
      responses:
        '400':
          x-ax-error-code: INVALID_PAYMENT_REQUEST
          x-ax-error-suggestion: Check amount is positive and currency is valid ISO 4217 code

docs.title: Schema
docs.text: Extensions are validated against the x-ax-extensions.schema.json schema.

docs.title: x-ax-capabilities
docs.text: Semantic capabilities for agent operation discovery.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Agents need to discover what operations are available and what they do. Capabilities provide:

  • Machine-readable operation semantics
  • Grouping by functional area
  • Discoverability for tool selection

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
x-ax-capabilities:
  type: array
  items:
    type: string
    pattern: "^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$"

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - list_users
        - search_users
    post:
      operationId: createUser
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - create_user

  /users/{id}:
    get:
      operationId: getUser
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - read_user
        - get_user_details
    delete:
      operationId: deleteUser
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - delete_user
        - remove_user

docs.title: Generated Code
docs.text:
type Capability string

const (
    CapListUsers   Capability = "list_users"
    CapSearchUsers Capability = "search_users"
    CapCreateUser  Capability = "create_user"
    CapReadUser    Capability = "read_user"
    CapDeleteUser  Capability = "delete_user"
)

// OperationsForCapability returns operations that provide a capability.
var OperationsForCapability = map[Capability][]string{
    CapListUsers:   {"listUsers"},
    CapSearchUsers: {"listUsers"},
    CapCreateUser:  {"createUser"},
    CapReadUser:    {"getUser"},
    CapDeleteUser:  {"deleteUser"},
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use snake_case — Consistent with the pattern requirement
  2. Be specific — create_payment is better than create
  3. Include aliases — Multiple capabilities can map to one operation
  4. Group by domain — Prefix with domain area (e.g., payment_, user_)

docs.title: x-ax-error-code
docs.text: Machine-readable error code for agent error classification.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Human-readable error messages are insufficient for agents. Error codes enable:

  • Programmatic error handling
  • Automatic retry decisions
  • Self-healing workflows
  • Error categorization

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
x-ax-error-code:
  type: string
  pattern: "^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$"

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: createPayment
      responses:
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
          x-ax-error-code: INVALID_PAYMENT_REQUEST
          x-ax-error-suggestion: Check amount is positive and currency is valid ISO 4217 code
        '402':
          description: Insufficient funds
          x-ax-error-code: INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
          x-ax-error-suggestion: Add funds to the account or use a different payment method
        '429':
          description: Rate limited
          x-ax-error-code: RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
          x-ax-error-suggestion: Wait and retry with exponential backoff

docs.title: Generated Code
docs.text:
// Error codes
const (
    ErrInvalidPaymentRequest = "INVALID_PAYMENT_REQUEST"
    ErrInsufficientFunds     = "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    ErrRateLimitExceeded     = "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
)

// ErrorMetadata provides details for error handling
var ErrorMetadata = map[string]struct {
    HTTPStatus int
    Retryable  bool
    Category   string
}{
    ErrInvalidPaymentRequest: {400, false, "validation"},
    ErrInsufficientFunds:     {402, false, "business"},
    ErrRateLimitExceeded:     {429, true, "rate_limit"},
}

docs.title: Error Categories
docs.text: Category Description Retryable validation Invalid input No authentication Auth failure No authorization Permission denied No not_found Resource missing No business Business rule violation No rate_limit Too many requests Yes (with backoff) server Internal error Yes unavailable Service down Yes

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE — Consistent with the pattern requirement
  2. Be specific — INVALID_DATE_RANGE is better than INVALID_INPUT
  3. Include suggestions — Pair with x-ax-error-suggestion
  4. Map to HTTP status — Error codes should align with response codes

docs.title: x-ax-required-fields
docs.text: Explicit list of fields required for deterministic operation execution.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
While OpenAPI schemas define required fields, agents benefit from a consolidated list at the operation level that includes:

  • Request body fields
  • Path parameters
  • Query parameters
  • Header requirements

This enables pre-flight validation before making API calls.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
x-ax-required-fields:
  type: array
  items:
    type: string

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: createPayment
      x-ax-required-fields:
        - amount
        - currency
        - recipient_id
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - amount
                - currency
                - recipient_id
              properties:
                amount:
                  type: number
                currency:
                  type: string
                recipient_id:
                  type: string

docs.title: Generated Code
docs.text:
// RequiredFieldsForOperation returns the required fields for an operation.
var RequiredFieldsForOperation = map[string][]string{
    "createPayment": {"amount", "currency", "recipient_id"},
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Include all required inputs — Not just request body, but also required path/query params
  2. Use consistent naming — Match the field names in your schema
  3. Order by importance — Put the most important fields first

docs.title: x-ax-retryable
docs.text: Indicates whether an operation is safe to retry on failure.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Agents need to know whether failed operations can be safely retried. This prevents:

  • Duplicate mutations (double-charging, duplicate records)
  • Data corruption
  • Wasted resources on non-retryable failures

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
x-ax-retryable:
  type: boolean
  default: false

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      x-ax-retryable: true  # Safe - read operation
    post:
      operationId: createUser
      x-ax-retryable: false  # Unsafe - may create duplicate

  /payments/{id}:
    get:
      operationId: getPayment
      x-ax-retryable: true  # Safe - read operation
    put:
      operationId: updatePayment
      x-ax-retryable: true  # Safe - idempotent update
      x-ax-idempotent: true

docs.title: Generated Code
docs.text:
// RetryPolicy defines retry behavior for an operation
type RetryPolicy struct {
    Retryable    bool
    MaxRetries   int
    BackoffMs    int
    BackoffScale float64
}

// RetryPolicies maps operations to their retry behavior
var RetryPolicies = map[string]RetryPolicy{
    "listUsers":     {true, 3, 1000, 2.0},
    "createUser":    {false, 0, 0, 0},
    "getPayment":    {true, 3, 1000, 2.0},
    "updatePayment": {true, 3, 1000, 2.0},
}

docs.title: Decision Matrix
docs.text:
HTTP Method Idempotent Retryable GET Yes Yes HEAD Yes Yes OPTIONS Yes Yes PUT Yes* Yes* DELETE Yes* Yes* POST No No PATCH No No

*Depends on implementation — use x-ax-idempotent to override

docs.title: Related Extensions
docs.text:
• x-ax-idempotent — Whether repeated calls produce the same result
  • x-ax-error-code — Error codes may indicate retry eligibility (e.g., RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED)

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Default to false — Safe by default
  2. Mark all reads as retryable — GET, HEAD, OPTIONS
  3. Consider idempotency — PUT/DELETE may be retryable if idempotent
  4. Never retry non-idempotent mutations — POST, most PATCH

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: The ax-spec CLI provides commands for linting, enriching, and generating code from OpenAPI specifications.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/plexusone/ax-spec/cmd/ax-spec@latest

docs.title: lint
docs.text:
Check OpenAPI specs against AX rules.

ax-spec lint <openapi-spec> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --level Compliance level (l1, l2, l3) --format Output format (text, json)

Example:

ax-spec lint api.yaml --level l2

docs.title: enrich
docs.text:
Add x-ax-* extensions to OpenAPI specs.

ax-spec enrich <openapi-spec> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description -o, --output Output file (default: stdout) --discover Make API calls to discover error codes --api-base API base URL for discovery --api-token API token for discovery --dry-run Preview changes without writing

Example:

# Infer extensions from spec structure
ax-spec enrich api.yaml -o api-ax.yaml

# Also discover via API calls
ax-spec enrich api.yaml -o api-ax.yaml \
  --discover \
  --api-base https://api.example.com \
  --api-token $API_TOKEN

docs.title: gen
docs.text:
Generate Go code from x-ax-* extensions.

ax-spec gen <openapi-spec> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description -o, --output Output directory (default: .) -p, --package Go package name (default: ax) --only Generate specific files (errors,retry,capabilities) --dry-run Preview what would be generated

Example:

ax-spec gen api-ax.yaml -o pkg/ax --package ax

Generated Files:

File Description errors.go Error code constants and metadata retry.go Retry policy mappings capabilities.go Operation capability mappings validation.go Required field validators

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description API_TOKEN Default API token for --discover

docs.title: Compliance Levels
docs.text: AX Spec defines three progressive compliance levels for incremental adoption.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Level Name Description AX-L1 Structured Valid OpenAPI with explicit types and operation IDs AX-L2 Deterministic Strict schemas, explicit required fields, no ambiguous types AX-L3 Agent-Ready Full AX extensions, structured errors, capability discovery

docs.title: AX-L1: Structured
docs.text: Minimum requirements for any API agents might consume.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• All schemas have explicit type fields
  • All operations have operationId and summary
  • Parameters declare required explicitly

docs.title: Ruleset
docs.text: vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/profiles/ax-l1-structured.yaml api.yaml

docs.title: AX-L2: Deterministic
docs.text: Strict typing for predictable agent behavior.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Everything in AX-L1, plus:

  • additionalProperties: false on object schemas
  • x-ax-required-fields on operations
  • Request bodies explicitly set required

docs.title: Ruleset
docs.text: vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/profiles/ax-l2-deterministic.yaml api.yaml

docs.title: AX-L3: Agent-Ready
docs.text: Full agent compatibility with error recovery.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Everything in AX-L2, plus:

  • x-ax-capabilities for operation discovery
  • x-ax-error-code on error responses
  • x-ax-retryable and x-ax-idempotent flags
  • Error responses have defined schemas

docs.title: Ruleset
docs.text: vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/profiles/ax-l3-agent-ready.yaml api.yaml

docs.title: Choosing a Level
docs.text:
If you... Start with Have an existing API AX-L1 Are building a new API AX-L2 Are building for AI agents AX-L3

Most teams should aim for AX-L2 as a baseline, with AX-L3 for agent-facing endpoints.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get started with AX Spec in minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21+ (for CLI)
  • Vacuum or Spectral (for linting)

docs.title: CLI
docs.text: go install github.com/plexusone/ax-spec/cmd/ax-spec@latest

docs.title: Ruleset Only
docs.text:
No installation needed — reference the ruleset URL directly:

vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/ax-openapi.json your-api.yaml

docs.title: 1. Lint Your OpenAPI Spec
docs.text:
ax-spec lint openapi.yaml

This checks your spec against AX rules and reports issues by DIRECT principle.

docs.title: 2. Enrich with AX Extensions
docs.text:
ax-spec enrich openapi.yaml -o openapi-ax.yaml

This adds x-ax-* extensions based on spec analysis:

  • x-ax-capabilities from operation IDs
  • x-ax-required-fields from schema requirements
  • x-ax-retryable based on HTTP method

docs.title: 3. Generate Go Code
docs.text:
ax-spec gen openapi-ax.yaml -o pkg/ax --package ax

This generates Go code from the extensions:

  • errors.go — Error code constants
  • retry.go — Retry policy mappings
  • capabilities.go — Operation capabilities
  • validation.go — Required field validators

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Compliance Levels — Understand AX-L1, AX-L2, AX-L3
  • CLI Reference — Full command documentation
  • Case Studies — Real-world implementations

docs.title: Rules Reference
docs.text: AX Spec provides Spectral/Vacuum rules organized by the DIRECT principles.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Principle Rules Focus Deterministic 3 Strict schemas, explicit types Introspectable 3 Operation metadata, capabilities Recoverable 4 Error handling, retry semantics Explicit 3 Required fields, constraints Consistent 2 Patterns, conventions Testable 2 Sandbox safety, examples

docs.title: With Vacuum
docs.text: vacuum lint --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/ax-openapi.json api.yaml

docs.title: With Spectral
docs.text: spectral lint api.yaml --ruleset https://raw.githubusercontent.com/plexusone/ax-spec/main/rules/ax-openapi.json

docs.title: Severity Levels
docs.text: Severity Description error Must fix — blocks agent usage warn Should fix — degrades agent experience info Consider — improves agent experience

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Rule Severity Description ax-deterministic-schema-type error Schemas must have explicit type ax-deterministic-required-fields warn Operations should declare x-ax-required-fields ax-introspectable-operation-id error Operations must have operationId ax-introspectable-summary error Operations must have summary ax-recoverable-error-schema error Error responses must have schemas ax-recoverable-error-structure warn Errors should have x-ax-error-code ax-explicit-request-body-required warn Request bodies must set required

docs.title: Consistent Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring uniform patterns across endpoints.

docs.title: ax-consistent-response-content-type
docs.text:
Severity: info

Responses should use application/json.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Consistent content types simplify agent implementation. JSON is the standard for API responses and has universal parsing support.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
responses:
  '200':
    content:
      text/plain:
        schema:
          type: string

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
responses:
  '200':
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            message:
              type: string

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text:
Some endpoints legitimately return non-JSON content:

  • File downloads (application/octet-stream)
  • Images (image/png, image/jpeg)
  • HTML pages (text/html)

For these, the rule is informational.

docs.title: ax-consistent-pagination
docs.text:
Severity: info

List operations should have pagination parameters.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Unpaginated list endpoints can return unbounded data, causing:

  • Memory issues in agents
  • Timeout errors
  • Rate limiting

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
get:
  operationId: listUsers
  parameters: []

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
get:
  operationId: listUsers
  parameters:
    - name: limit
      in: query
      required: false
      schema:
        type: integer
        default: 20
        maximum: 100
    - name: offset
      in: query
      required: false
      schema:
        type: integer
        default: 0

docs.title: Alternative: Cursor-based
docs.text:
parameters:
  - name: cursor
    in: query
    required: false
    schema:
      type: string
  - name: limit
    in: query
    required: false
    schema:
      type: integer
      default: 20

docs.title: Deterministic Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring predictable behavior with strict schemas.

docs.title: ax-deterministic-schema-type
docs.text:
Severity: error

Schema properties must have explicit type fields.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Agents cannot handle dynamic typing. Without explicit types, generated code falls back to interface{} or any, losing type safety.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
properties:
  data:
    description: The response data

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
properties:
  data:
    type: object
    description: The response data

docs.title: ax-deterministic-required-fields
docs.text:
Severity: warn

Operations should declare x-ax-required-fields.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Agents need to know which fields are required before making API calls. This enables pre-flight validation and better error messages.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createUser
  requestBody:
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createUser
  x-ax-required-fields:
    - email
    - name
  requestBody:
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'

docs.title: ax-deterministic-no-additional-properties
docs.text:
Severity: warn

Object schemas should set additionalProperties: false.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Without this, APIs can return unexpected fields that agents don't know how to handle. Strict schemas ensure predictable responses.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
User:
  type: object
  properties:
    id:
      type: string
    name:
      type: string

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
User:
  type: object
  additionalProperties: false
  properties:
    id:
      type: string
    name:
      type: string

docs.title: Explicit Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring all constraints are declared in the specification.

docs.title: ax-explicit-request-body-required
docs.text:
Severity: warn

Request bodies must set required explicitly.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Ambiguity about whether a request body is required leads to agent errors. Explicit declaration enables proper validation.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createUser
  requestBody:
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createUser
  requestBody:
    required: true
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'

docs.title: ax-explicit-parameter-required
docs.text:
Severity: warn

Parameters must set required explicitly.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Implicit optionality causes agent confusion. Be explicit about whether parameters are required.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
parameters:
  - name: limit
    in: query
    schema:
      type: integer

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
parameters:
  - name: limit
    in: query
    required: false
    schema:
      type: integer
      default: 20

docs.title: ax-explicit-enum-values
docs.text:
Severity: info

Constrained strings should use enum.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Free-form strings that only accept certain values should declare those values as an enum. This enables:

  • Agent validation before API calls
  • Code generation with type-safe constants
  • Documentation of valid values

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
properties:
  status:
    type: string
    description: One of "pending", "completed", or "failed"

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
properties:
  status:
    type: string
    enum:
      - pending
      - completed
      - failed

docs.title: Introspectable Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring machine-readable capabilities and metadata.

docs.title: ax-introspectable-operation-id
docs.text:
Severity: error

Operations must have operationId.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Operation IDs are essential for:

  • Code generation (function names)
  • Agent tool selection
  • Logging and tracing
  • Documentation cross-references

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      summary: List users

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      summary: List users

docs.title: ax-introspectable-summary
docs.text:
Severity: error

Operations must have summary.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Summaries help agents understand what an operation does. They're used for:

  • Tool descriptions in agent prompts
  • Documentation generation
  • API discovery

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
paths:
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      summary: List all users with optional filtering

docs.title: ax-introspectable-capabilities
docs.text:
Severity: info

Operations should declare x-ax-capabilities.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Capabilities enable agents to discover operations by intent rather than by name. An agent looking for "how to create a payment" can find operations with create_payment capability.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: createPayment
      summary: Create a payment

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
paths:
  /payments:
    post:
      operationId: createPayment
      summary: Create a payment
      x-ax-capabilities:
        - create_payment
        - transfer_funds

docs.title: Recoverable Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring structured errors with actionable suggestions.

docs.title: ax-recoverable-error-schema
docs.text:
Severity: error

Error responses must have a defined schema.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Without schemas, agents receive unstructured error responses they cannot parse programmatically. This prevents automated error recovery.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
responses:
  '400':
    description: Bad request

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
responses:
  '400':
    description: Bad request
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'

docs.title: ax-recoverable-error-structure
docs.text:
Severity: warn

Error responses should include x-ax-error-code.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Human-readable error messages are insufficient for agents. Machine-readable error codes enable:

  • Programmatic error handling
  • Automatic retry decisions
  • Self-healing workflows

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
responses:
  '400':
    description: Invalid payment amount
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
responses:
  '400':
    description: Invalid payment amount
    x-ax-error-code: INVALID_PAYMENT_AMOUNT
    x-ax-error-suggestion: Ensure amount is positive and less than account limit
    content:
      application/json:
        schema:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'

docs.title: ax-recoverable-retryable
docs.text:
Severity: info

Mutating operations should indicate retry semantics with x-ax-retryable.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Agents need to know whether failed operations can be safely retried to avoid duplicate mutations.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createPayment

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createPayment
  x-ax-retryable: false

docs.title: ax-recoverable-idempotent
docs.text:
Severity: info

PUT/DELETE operations should indicate idempotency with x-ax-idempotent.

docs.title: Why
docs.text: Idempotent operations can be safely retried. Agents need this information for reliable error recovery.

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
put:
  operationId: updateUser

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
put:
  operationId: updateUser
  x-ax-idempotent: true
  x-ax-retryable: true

docs.title: Testable Rules
docs.text: Rules ensuring safe sandbox environments for agent experimentation.

docs.title: ax-testable-sandboxable
docs.text:
Severity: info

Operations should indicate sandbox safety with x-ax-sandboxable.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Agents need to experiment with APIs safely. Knowing which operations can be called in test/sandbox environments enables:

  • Safe exploration during development
  • Automated testing without side effects
  • Confidence in agent behavior

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createPayment

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
post:
  operationId: createPayment
  x-ax-sandboxable: true
  x-ax-retryable: false

docs.title: Guidelines
docs.text: Operation Type Sandboxable Read operations Yes Test/mock endpoints Yes Reversible operations Yes Financial transactions Depends on sandbox mode External integrations Usually no Irreversible mutations Usually no

docs.title: ax-testable-example-values
docs.text:
Severity: info

Properties should have example values.

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Examples help agents understand:

  • Expected data formats
  • Reasonable default values
  • Valid input ranges

docs.title: Bad
docs.text:
properties:
  email:
    type: string
    format: email
  created_at:
    type: string
    format: date-time

docs.title: Good
docs.text:
properties:
  email:
    type: string
    format: email
    example: "user@example.com"
  created_at:
    type: string
    format: date-time
    example: "2024-01-15T09:30:00Z"

docs.title: Schema-Level Examples
docs.text:
For complex objects, use schema-level examples:

User:
  type: object
  properties:
    id:
      type: string
    name:
      type: string
    email:
      type: string
  example:
    id: "usr_123"
    name: "Jane Doe"
    email: "jane@example.com"
